title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code working repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ntistools public R package wrangling Nonprofit Trends Impacts Survey (NTIS) data, developed Urban Institute. provides pipe-friendly data-cleaning functions replace repetitive dplyr::case_when() / dplyr::across() patterns common NTIS analysis scripts. public repo. Never commit raw survey data, PII, internal file paths, credentials.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Install package locally (required before running tests) R CMD INSTALL .  # Run all tests (package must be installed first) Rscript -e 'library(testthat); library(ntistools); testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")'  # Run a single test file Rscript -e 'library(testthat); library(ntistools); testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-recode_binary.R")'  # Rebuild documentation (roxygen2 -> man/ and NAMESPACE) Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()'  # Full package check (if devtools is available) Rscript -e 'devtools::check()'

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Standard R package layout using roxygen2 workflow: R/: Exported functions, one per file. takes data frame first argument returns modified data frame (pipe-friendly). man/: Auto-generated roxygen2 comments. Never edit directly. NAMESPACE: Auto-generated roxygen2. Never edit directly. tests/testthat/: One test file per function (test-{function_name}.R). Uses testthat edition 3. vignettes/: --.Rmd — side--side comparisons raw dplyr vs ntistools. inst/examples/: Example NTIS cleaning scripts showing patterns functions replace. **_pkgdown.yml**: pkgdown site config. Site auto-deploys gh-pages push main. .github/workflows/: GitHub Actions pkgdown deployment.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Dependencies: dplyr rlang. Keep minimal. Column selection: functions accept column names character vectors via vars parameter (used dplyr::all_of()). Exception: combine_binary count_binary use ... unquoted tidyselect names. Survey sentinel values: NTIS uses numeric sentinels: 98 = “don’t know”, 97 = “applicable”, 0 = “none”. Many function defaults reflect . NA propagation: source columns NA, derived columns also NA (0). deliberate across combine_binary, count_binary, apply_filter. roxygen2 markdown: Enabled via Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) DESCRIPTION. rlang imports: .data := imported rlang R/ntistools-package.R.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Create R/{function_name}.R roxygen2 documentation @export tag Create tests/testthat/test-{function_name}.R testthat tests Run Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()' update NAMESPACE man/ Run R CMD INSTALL . run tests verify Add /section vignettes/--.Rmd Add function appropriate group _pkgdown.yml

title: CLAUDE.md
text: .gitignore blocks *.csv, *.xlsx, *.sav, *.dta, *.rds, *.rda, data/, data-raw/, .env Example scripts inst/examples/ must use placeholder paths, never real internal paths commit prompts/ directory (gitignored)

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: NTIS data-cleaning scripts tend repeat case_when() patterns : combining binary indicators, removing sentinels, collapsing Likert scales, labeling columns, . typical script might contain 20+ blocks nearly identical code like : ntistools replaces pattern single, purpose-built function scripts shorter, easier read, less error-prone. vignette walks function, showing verbose “” code alongside concise “” equivalent, using small synthetic dataset.
code: # This same pattern, repeated for every binary variable... ProgDem_Veterans = case_when(   ProgDem_Veterans == 1 ~ "Serving veterans",   ProgDem_Veterans == 0 ~ "Not serving veterans",   .default = NA_character_ )

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: start creating synthetic dataset mimics structure real NTIS survey data. column represents common pattern ’ll encounter:
code: library(ntistools) library(dplyr)  survey <- data.frame(   # Binary geographic indicators (0 = no, 1 = yes)   GeoAreas_Local          = c(1, 0, 0, NA, 0, 1),   GeoAreas_MultipleLocal  = c(0, 0, 1, NA, 0, 0),   GeoAreas_RegionalWithin = c(0, 0, 0, NA, 0, 0),   GeoAreas_MultipleState  = c(0, NA, 1, NA, 0, 0),   GeoAreas_RegionalAcross = c(0, NA, 0, NA, 0, 1),    # Disruption indicators (0 = no, 1 = yes)   LLLost  = c(1, 0, 1, NA, 0, 1),   LLDelay = c(1, 1, 0, NA, 0, 1),   LLStop  = c(0, 0, 0, NA, 0, 1),    # ProgDem-style columns (0 = no, 1 = yes primary, 2 = yes secondary, 98 = NA)   ProgDem_Children = c(0, 1, 2, 98, 0, 1),   ProgDem_Elders   = c(1, 0, 98, 2, 0, 1),    # Sentinel values (98 = "don't know")   DonImportance = c(3, 1, 98, 2, 98, 4),    # Flag-based imputation columns   # PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X = 1 means "respondent confirmed they had zero people   # waiting", so NA should be replaced with 0   PplSrv_NumWait      = c(50, NA, 10, NA, 30, NA),   PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X = c(0,   1,  0,  0,  0,  1),    # Imputation with year-suffixed variable names   # The variable has a year suffix but the flag column does not   Staff_RegVlntr_2023 = c(NA, 5,  NA, 10, NA, 3),   Staff_RegVlntr_2024 = c(2,  NA, NA, 7,  NA, 4),   Staff_RegVlntr_NA   = c(1,  0,  0,  0,  1,  0),    # Likert scales (1-5, with 97 = "not applicable")   FinanceChng_Benefits  = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 97),   FinanceChng_Salaries  = c(5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 97),   FinanceChng_TotExp    = c(2, 3, 97, 5, 1, 4),    # Finance change indicators (1 = yes, 0 = no, 97 = not applicable)   FinanceChng_Reserves = c(1, 0, 97, 1, 0, 1),   PrgSrvc_Suspend      = c(1, 0, 97, 1, 0, 0),    # Parent/child regulation pattern   Regulations         = c(1, 0, 97, 1, 0, 1),   Regulations_Federal = c(1, NA, NA, 0, NA, 1),   Regulations_State   = c(0, NA, NA, 1, NA, 1),    # Staff filter columns   HaveStaff      = c(1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1),   StaffVacancies = c(3, 1, 2, 0, 4, 1),   BenefitsImpact = c(2, 3, 1, 4, 2, 3) )

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: several related 0/1 columns need single column 1 1. example, NTIS extraction script, GeoAreas_Local, GeoAreas_MultipleLocal, GeoAreas_RegionalWithin combined GeoAreas_ServedLocal.

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     GeoAreas_Locally = case_when(       GeoAreas_Local == 1 | GeoAreas_MultipleLocal == 1 |         GeoAreas_RegionalWithin == 1 ~ 1,       is.na(GeoAreas_Local) & is.na(GeoAreas_MultipleLocal) &         is.na(GeoAreas_RegionalWithin) ~ NA,       .default = 0     )   )  before %>% select(starts_with("GeoAreas_L"), GeoAreas_MultipleLocal,                   GeoAreas_RegionalWithin) #>   GeoAreas_Local GeoAreas_Locally GeoAreas_MultipleLocal #> 1              1                1                      0 #> 2              0                0                      0 #> 3              0                1                      1 #> 4             NA               NA                     NA #> 5              0                0                      0 #> 6              1                1                      0 #>   GeoAreas_RegionalWithin #> 1                       0 #> 2                       0 #> 3                       0 #> 4                      NA #> 5                       0 #> 6                       0

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   combine_binary(GeoAreas_Locally,                  GeoAreas_Local, GeoAreas_MultipleLocal,                  GeoAreas_RegionalWithin)  after %>% select(starts_with("GeoAreas_L"), GeoAreas_MultipleLocal,                  GeoAreas_RegionalWithin) #>   GeoAreas_Local GeoAreas_Locally GeoAreas_MultipleLocal #> 1              1                1                      0 #> 2              0                0                      0 #> 3              0                1                      1 #> 4             NA               NA                     NA #> 5              0                0                      0 #> 6              1                1                      0 #>   GeoAreas_RegionalWithin #> 1                       0 #> 2                       0 #> 3                       0 #> 4                      NA #> 5                       0 #> 6                       0

what: Option: strict_na for stricter NA handling
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: default, combine_binary() returns NA source columns NA. row c(0, NA), result 0. Sometimes need stricter behavior: source NA (none 1), result NA. matches binary_flag() function used older NTIS scripts. Set strict_na = TRUE get behavior. use strict_na = TRUE: Use missing value means genuinely don’t know respondent answered “yes.” NTIS extraction script, GeoAreas_ServedLocal GeoAreas_Servedmultistate variables use strict mode.
code: # Data where one source is 0 and the other is NA strict_example <- data.frame(   a = c(0,  1,  NA, 0),   b = c(NA, NA, NA, 0) )  # Default: lenient (0 + NA = 0) combine_binary(strict_example, result_lenient, a, b) %>%   select(a, b, result_lenient) #>    a  b result_lenient #> 1  0 NA              0 #> 2  1 NA              1 #> 3 NA NA             NA #> 4  0  0              0  # Strict: any NA poisons the result (0 + NA = NA), unless a 1 is present combine_binary(strict_example, result_strict, a, b, strict_na = TRUE) %>%   select(a, b, result_strict) #>    a  b result_strict #> 1  0 NA            NA #> 2  1 NA             1 #> 3 NA NA            NA #> 4  0  0             0

what: 2. count_binary() — Any / count / all from binary indicators
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Creates three summary columns set binary columns: {prefix}_any: 1 source 1 {prefix}_count: many sources 1 {prefix}_all: 1 every source 1 three NA every source column NA.

previous_headings: 2. count_binary() — Any / count / all from binary indicators
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     LLDisruption_any = case_when(       LLLost == 1 | LLDelay == 1 | LLStop == 1 ~ 1,       is.na(LLLost) & is.na(LLDelay) & is.na(LLStop) ~ NA,       .default = 0     ),     LLDisruption_count = case_when(       is.na(LLLost) & is.na(LLDelay) & is.na(LLStop) ~ NA_real_,       .default = rowSums(across(c(LLLost, LLDelay, LLStop),                                 ~ . == 1), na.rm = TRUE)     ),     LLDisruption_all = case_when(       LLLost == 1 & LLDelay == 1 & LLStop == 1 ~ 1,       is.na(LLLost) & is.na(LLDelay) & is.na(LLStop) ~ NA,       .default = 0     )   )  before %>% select(starts_with("LL")) #>   LLLost LLDelay LLStop LLDisruption_any LLDisruption_count LLDisruption_all #> 1      1       1      0                1                  2                0 #> 2      0       1      0                1                  1                0 #> 3      1       0      0                1                  1                0 #> 4     NA      NA     NA               NA                 NA               NA #> 5      0       0      0                0                  0                0 #> 6      1       1      1                1                  3                1

previous_headings: 2. count_binary() — Any / count / all from binary indicators
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Tip: need _any column (_count _all), use combine_binary() instead — creates single column lets name whatever want.
code: after <- survey %>%   count_binary("LLDisruption", LLLost, LLDelay, LLStop)  after %>% select(starts_with("LL")) #>   LLLost LLDelay LLStop LLDisruption_any LLDisruption_count LLDisruption_all #> 1      1       1      0                1                  2                0 #> 2      0       1      0                1                  1                0 #> 3      1       0      0                1                  1                0 #> 4     NA      NA     NA               NA                 NA               NA #> 5      0       0      0                0                  0                0 #> 6      1       1      1                1                  3                1

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: NTIS demographic columns use 0 = “”, 1 = “yes (primary)”, 2 = “yes (secondary)”, 98 = “don’t know.” need collapse simple 0/1. extraction script, ProgDem_ columns go step.

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: recode_progdem <- function(x) {   case_when(     x == 0 ~ 0, x == 1 ~ 1, x == 2 ~ 1,     x == 98 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ NA_real_   ) }  before <- survey %>%   mutate(across(c(ProgDem_Children, ProgDem_Elders),                 recode_progdem))  before %>% select(starts_with("ProgDem")) #>   ProgDem_Children ProgDem_Elders #> 1                0              1 #> 2                1              0 #> 3                1             NA #> 4               NA              1 #> 5                0              0 #> 6                1              1

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   recode_binary(c("ProgDem_Children", "ProgDem_Elders"))  after %>% select(starts_with("ProgDem")) #>   ProgDem_Children ProgDem_Elders #> 1                0              1 #> 2                1              0 #> 3                1             NA #> 4               NA              1 #> 5                0              0 #> 6                1              1

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: defaults (ones = c(1, 2), zeros = 0, na_values = 98) work ProgDem_ columns, can customize patterns:
code: # Example: a column where 1-3 mean "yes" and 4-5 mean "no" custom_example <- data.frame(rating = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 98))  recode_binary(custom_example, "rating",               ones = c(1, 2, 3),               zeros = c(4, 5),               na_values = 98) %>%   pull(rating) #> [1]  1  1  1  0  0 NA

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Replace survey sentinel values (e.g., 98 = “don’t know”) NA. extraction script removes 98s dozens columns analysis.

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(across(     all_of("DonImportance"),     ~ case_when(. == 98 ~ NA, TRUE ~ .)   ))  before %>% select(DonImportance) #>   DonImportance #> 1             3 #> 2             1 #> 3            NA #> 4             2 #> 5            NA #> 6             4

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   recode_sentinel("DonImportance")  after %>% select(DonImportance) #>   DonImportance #> 1             3 #> 2             1 #> 3            NA #> 4             2 #> 5            NA #> 6             4

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: default, recode_sentinel() removes 98. can change values remove:
code: # Remove both 97 and 98 survey %>%   recode_sentinel("DonImportance", values = c(97, 98)) %>%   select(DonImportance) #>   DonImportance #> 1             3 #> 2             1 #> 3            NA #> 4             2 #> 5            NA #> 6             4

what: 5. impute_from_flag() — Fill NA when a flag says “skip was valid”
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: respondent legitimately skipped numeric question (e.g., “many people waitlist?” waitlist), value NA companion flag column (e.g., PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X) set 1. NAs replaced 0 (left missing data).

previous_headings: 5. impute_from_flag() — Fill NA when a flag says “skip was valid”
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     PplSrv_NumWait = case_when(       PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X == 1 & is.na(PplSrv_NumWait) ~ 0,       .default = PplSrv_NumWait     )   )  before %>% select(starts_with("PplSrv")) #>   PplSrv_NumWait PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X #> 1             50                   0 #> 2              0                   1 #> 3             10                   0 #> 4             NA                   0 #> 5             30                   0 #> 6              0                   1

previous_headings: 5. impute_from_flag() — Fill NA when a flag says “skip was valid”
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   impute_from_flag("PplSrv_NumWait")  after %>% select(starts_with("PplSrv")) #>   PplSrv_NumWait PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X #> 1             50                   0 #> 2              0                   1 #> 3             10                   0 #> 4             NA                   0 #> 5             30                   0 #> 6              0                   1

previous_headings: 5. impute_from_flag() — Fill NA when a flag says “skip was valid”
what: Option: flag_map for non-standard flag column names
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Sometimes variable name includes year suffix (e.g., Staff_RegVlntr_2023) flag column (e.g., Staff_RegVlntr_NA). default paste0(var, flag_suffix) logic look Staff_RegVlntr_2023_NA_X, doesn’t exist. Use flag_map tell function exactly flag column use variable: Tip: can mix flag_map default suffix call. Variables listed flag_map use mapped flag; others fall back paste0(var, flag_suffix).
code: # Before: six separate case_when blocks in the extraction script before <- survey %>%   mutate(     Staff_RegVlntr_2023 = case_when(       is.na(Staff_RegVlntr_2023) & Staff_RegVlntr_NA == 1 ~ 0,       .default = Staff_RegVlntr_2023     ),     Staff_RegVlntr_2024 = case_when(       is.na(Staff_RegVlntr_2024) & Staff_RegVlntr_NA == 1 ~ 0,       .default = Staff_RegVlntr_2024     )   )  before %>% select(starts_with("Staff_RegVlntr")) #>   Staff_RegVlntr_2023 Staff_RegVlntr_2024 Staff_RegVlntr_NA #> 1                   0                   2                 1 #> 2                   5                  NA                 0 #> 3                  NA                  NA                 0 #> 4                  10                   7                 0 #> 5                   0                   0                 1 #> 6                   3                   4                 0 # After: one call handles both year columns after <- survey %>%   impute_from_flag(     vars = c("Staff_RegVlntr_2023", "Staff_RegVlntr_2024"),     flag_map = c(Staff_RegVlntr_2023 = "Staff_RegVlntr_NA",                  Staff_RegVlntr_2024 = "Staff_RegVlntr_NA")   )  after %>% select(starts_with("Staff_RegVlntr")) #>   Staff_RegVlntr_2023 Staff_RegVlntr_2024 Staff_RegVlntr_NA #> 1                   0                   2                 1 #> 2                   5                  NA                 0 #> 3                  NA                  NA                 0 #> 4                  10                   7                 0 #> 5                   0                   0                 1 #> 6                   3                   4                 0

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Collapse 5-point Likert scale (1–5) 3 categories (1 = low, 2 = mid, 3 = high), 97 set NA. default mapping = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 3) means:

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     FinanceChng_Benefits = case_match(       FinanceChng_Benefits,       1 ~ 1, 2 ~ 1, 3 ~ 2, 4 ~ 3, 5 ~ 3, 97 ~ NA     )   ) #> Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`. #> ℹ In argument: `FinanceChng_Benefits = case_match(...)`. #> Caused by warning: #> ! `case_match()` was deprecated in dplyr 1.2.0. #> ℹ Please use `recode_values()` instead.  before %>% select(FinanceChng_Benefits) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits #> 1                    1 #> 2                    1 #> 3                    2 #> 4                    3 #> 5                    3 #> 6                   NA

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   collapse_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits")  after %>% select(FinanceChng_Benefits) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits #> 1                    1 #> 2                    1 #> 3                    2 #> 4                    3 #> 5                    3 #> 6                   NA

what: Applying to multiple columns at once
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: extraction script, many Likert columns collapsed using mapping. Pass character vector column names:
code: likert_cols <- c("FinanceChng_Benefits", "FinanceChng_Salaries",                  "FinanceChng_TotExp")  after <- survey %>%   collapse_likert(likert_cols)  after %>% select(all_of(likert_cols)) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits FinanceChng_Salaries FinanceChng_TotExp #> 1                    1                    3                  1 #> 2                    1                    3                  2 #> 3                    2                    2                 NA #> 4                    3                    1                  3 #> 5                    3                    1                  1 #> 6                   NA                   NA                  3

what: 7. propagate_parent() — Push parent value to children
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: parent question (e.g., “organization face government regulations?”) 0 (“”) 97 (“N/”), follow-questions inherit value instead left NA.

previous_headings: 7. propagate_parent() — Push parent value to children
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(across(     c(Regulations_Federal, Regulations_State),     ~ case_when(       Regulations == 0 ~ 0,       Regulations == 97 ~ 97,       TRUE ~ .     )   ))  before %>% select(starts_with("Regulations")) #>   Regulations Regulations_Federal Regulations_State #> 1           1                   1                 0 #> 2           0                   0                 0 #> 3          97                  97                97 #> 4           1                   0                 1 #> 5           0                   0                 0 #> 6           1                   1                 1

previous_headings: 7. propagate_parent() — Push parent value to children
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   propagate_parent(c("Regulations_Federal", "Regulations_State"),                    parent_var = "Regulations")  after %>% select(starts_with("Regulations")) #>   Regulations Regulations_Federal Regulations_State #> 1           1                   1                 0 #> 2           0                   0                 0 #> 3          97                  97                97 #> 4           1                   0                 1 #> 5           0                   0                 0 #> 6           1                   1                 1

previous_headings: 7. propagate_parent() — Push parent value to children
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: default, values = c(0, 97) (“” “applicable” propagate). can change :
code: # Only propagate 0 (not 97) survey %>%   propagate_parent(c("Regulations_Federal", "Regulations_State"),                    parent_var = "Regulations",                    values = 0) %>%   select(starts_with("Regulations")) #>   Regulations Regulations_Federal Regulations_State #> 1           1                   1                 0 #> 2           0                   0                 0 #> 3          97                  NA                NA #> 4           1                   0                 1 #> 5           0                   0                 0 #> 6           1                   1                 1

what: 8. apply_filter() — Set variables to NA for ineligible respondents
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: survey questions apply certain organizations. example, questions staff vacancies benefits make sense organizations paid staff. orgs without staff, values set NA don’t affect analysis.

previous_headings: 8. apply_filter() — Set variables to NA for ineligible respondents
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(across(     c(StaffVacancies, BenefitsImpact),     ~ case_when(HaveStaff == 1 ~ ., .default = NA)   ))  before %>% select(HaveStaff, StaffVacancies, BenefitsImpact) #>   HaveStaff StaffVacancies BenefitsImpact #> 1         1              3              2 #> 2         1              1              3 #> 3         0             NA             NA #> 4         1              0              4 #> 5         0             NA             NA #> 6         1              1              3

previous_headings: 8. apply_filter() — Set variables to NA for ineligible respondents
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: Tip: condition argument accepts expression use dplyr::filter(). example, condition = HaveStaff == 1 & year == "2024" keep values orgs staff 2024.
code: after <- survey %>%   apply_filter(c("StaffVacancies", "BenefitsImpact"),                condition = HaveStaff == 1)  after %>% select(HaveStaff, StaffVacancies, BenefitsImpact) #>   HaveStaff StaffVacancies BenefitsImpact #> 1         1              3              2 #> 2         1              1              3 #> 3         0             NA             NA #> 4         1              0              4 #> 5         0             NA             NA #> 6         1              1              3

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: recoding 0/1, often need convert columns descriptive strings reporting. NTIS extraction script 25+ blocks identical case_when() code like , one per variable. label_binary() replaces single call.

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     FinanceChng_Reserves = case_when(       FinanceChng_Reserves == 1 ~ "Drew on cash reserves",       FinanceChng_Reserves %in% c(0, 97) ~ "Did not draw on cash reserves",       .default = NA_character_     ),     PrgSrvc_Suspend = case_when(       PrgSrvc_Suspend == 1 ~ "Paused or suspended services",       PrgSrvc_Suspend %in% c(0, 97) ~ "Did not pause or suspend services",       .default = NA_character_     )   )  before %>% select(FinanceChng_Reserves, PrgSrvc_Suspend) #>            FinanceChng_Reserves                   PrgSrvc_Suspend #> 1         Drew on cash reserves      Paused or suspended services #> 2 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 3 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 4         Drew on cash reserves      Paused or suspended services #> 5 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 6         Drew on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   label_binary(     labels = list(       FinanceChng_Reserves = c("Drew on cash reserves",                                "Did not draw on cash reserves"),       PrgSrvc_Suspend = c("Paused or suspended services",                           "Did not pause or suspend services")     ),     na_values = 97   )  after %>% select(FinanceChng_Reserves, PrgSrvc_Suspend) #>            FinanceChng_Reserves                   PrgSrvc_Suspend #> 1         Drew on cash reserves      Paused or suspended services #> 2 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 3 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 4         Drew on cash reserves      Paused or suspended services #> 5 Did not draw on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services #> 6         Drew on cash reserves Did not pause or suspend services

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: labels argument named list. name column data, value length-2 character vector: c("label 1", "label 0").

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: NTIS variables treat 97 (“applicable”) 0 (“”). example, organization answered “applicable” whether drew cash reserves, effectively means . Set na_values = 97 map 97 false (second) label instead NA.
code: # Without na_values: 97 becomes NA survey %>%   label_binary(     labels = list(FinanceChng_Reserves = c("Drew", "Did not draw"))   ) %>%   select(FinanceChng_Reserves) #>   FinanceChng_Reserves #> 1                 Drew #> 2         Did not draw #> 3                 <NA> #> 4                 Drew #> 5         Did not draw #> 6                 Drew  # With na_values = 97: 97 maps to the false label survey %>%   label_binary(     labels = list(FinanceChng_Reserves = c("Drew", "Did not draw")),     na_values = 97   ) %>%   select(FinanceChng_Reserves) #>   FinanceChng_Reserves #> 1                 Drew #> 2         Did not draw #> 3         Did not draw #> 4                 Drew #> 5         Did not draw #> 6                 Drew

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: extraction script, 16 ProgDem_ columns labeled “Serving…” / “serving…” pair. can handle one call:
code: # First recode to 0/1 (2 = secondary yes), then label after <- survey %>%   recode_binary(c("ProgDem_Children", "ProgDem_Elders")) %>%   label_binary(labels = list(     ProgDem_Children = c("Serving children and youth",                          "Not serving children and youth"),     ProgDem_Elders   = c("Serving seniors",                          "Not serving seniors")   ))  after %>% select(starts_with("ProgDem")) #>                 ProgDem_Children      ProgDem_Elders #> 1 Not serving children and youth     Serving seniors #> 2     Serving children and youth Not serving seniors #> 3     Serving children and youth                <NA> #> 4                           <NA>     Serving seniors #> 5 Not serving children and youth Not serving seniors #> 6     Serving children and youth     Serving seniors

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: variables don’t use standard 0/1 coding:
code: # A column where 1,2 = "yes" and 3,4,5 = "no" age_example <- data.frame(Dem_BChair_Age = c(1, 2, 3, 5, 97, NA))  label_binary(age_example,   labels = list(Dem_BChair_Age = c("Under 35", "35 or older")),   true_values = c(1, 2),   false_values = c(3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9),   na_values = 97 ) %>%   pull(Dem_BChair_Age) #> [1] "Under 35"    "Under 35"    "35 or older" "35 or older" "35 or older" #> [6] NA

what: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: problem: (instead ) collapsing Likert values integers, need descriptive string labels reporting. extraction script 8+ across() blocks different column groups, slightly different labels. label_likert() uses mapping vector approach collapse_likert(), looks corresponding label. Values na_values get na_label (default "Unsure") instead NA.

previous_headings: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: before <- survey %>%   mutate(     FinanceChng_Benefits = case_when(       FinanceChng_Benefits %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Decrease",       FinanceChng_Benefits == 3 ~ "No change",       FinanceChng_Benefits %in% c(4, 5) ~ "Increase",       FinanceChng_Benefits == 97 ~ "Unsure",       .default = NA_character_     )   )  before %>% select(FinanceChng_Benefits) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits #> 1             Decrease #> 2             Decrease #> 3            No change #> 4             Increase #> 5             Increase #> 6               Unsure

previous_headings: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
code: after <- survey %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits",                labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase"))  after %>% select(FinanceChng_Benefits) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits #> 1             Decrease #> 2             Decrease #> 3            No change #> 4             Increase #> 5             Increase #> 6               Unsure

previous_headings: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
what: Applying different labels to different column groups
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: extraction script uses label_likert() several groups columns, different label text. can chain multiple calls:
code: after <- survey %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits",                labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase")) %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_Salaries",                labels = c("Decrease in salaries", "No change",                           "Increase in salaries")) %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_TotExp",                labels = c("Decrease in expenses", "No change",                           "Increase in expenses"))  after %>% select(starts_with("FinanceChng")) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits FinanceChng_Salaries   FinanceChng_TotExp #> 1             Decrease Increase in salaries Decrease in expenses #> 2             Decrease Increase in salaries            No change #> 3            No change            No change               Unsure #> 4             Increase Decrease in salaries Increase in expenses #> 5             Increase Decrease in salaries Decrease in expenses #> 6               Unsure               Unsure Increase in expenses #>   FinanceChng_Reserves #> 1                    1 #> 2                    0 #> 3                   97 #> 4                    1 #> 5                    0 #> 6                    1

previous_headings: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: default na_label "Unsure", can change :
code: survey %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits",                labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase"),                na_label = "Not applicable") %>%   select(FinanceChng_Benefits) #>   FinanceChng_Benefits #> 1             Decrease #> 2             Decrease #> 3            No change #> 4             Increase #> 5             Increase #> 6       Not applicable

previous_headings: 10. label_likert() — Label Likert scales with descriptive strings
title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: default mapping = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 3) collapses 5-point scale 3 categories. can provide different mapping scale different:
code: # 3-point scale: keep each value as its own category three_point <- data.frame(satisfaction = c(1, 2, 3, 97))  label_likert(three_point, "satisfaction",              labels = c("Dissatisfied", "Neutral", "Satisfied"),              mapping = c(1L, 2L, 3L)) %>%   pull(satisfaction) #> [1] "Dissatisfied" "Neutral"      "Satisfied"    "Unsure"

title: Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools
text: real NTIS cleaning script, chain multiple ntistools calls single pipeline. ’s condensed example showing functions work together: used hundreds lines repetitive case_when() calls becomes single, readable pipeline. step clear name tells , options explicit rather buried copy-pasted code.
code: cleaned <- survey %>%   # Step 1: Combine related binary indicators into summary columns   combine_binary(GeoAreas_Locally,                  GeoAreas_Local, GeoAreas_MultipleLocal,                  GeoAreas_RegionalWithin) %>%   combine_binary(GeoAreas_Multistate,                  GeoAreas_MultipleState, GeoAreas_RegionalAcross,                  strict_na = TRUE) %>%    # Step 2: Create any/count/all summaries for disruption indicators   count_binary("LLDisruption", LLLost, LLDelay, LLStop) %>%    # Step 3: Recode multi-level demographics to simple 0/1   recode_binary(c("ProgDem_Children", "ProgDem_Elders")) %>%    # Step 4: Remove sentinel values   recode_sentinel("DonImportance") %>%    # Step 5: Fill in valid-skip NAs using flag columns   impute_from_flag("PplSrv_NumWait") %>%   impute_from_flag(     vars = c("Staff_RegVlntr_2023", "Staff_RegVlntr_2024"),     flag_map = c(Staff_RegVlntr_2023 = "Staff_RegVlntr_NA",                  Staff_RegVlntr_2024 = "Staff_RegVlntr_NA")   ) %>%    # Step 6: Propagate parent answers to children   propagate_parent(c("Regulations_Federal", "Regulations_State"),                    parent_var = "Regulations") %>%    # Step 7: Zero out variables for ineligible respondents   apply_filter(c("StaffVacancies", "BenefitsImpact"),                condition = HaveStaff == 1) %>%    # Step 8: Label binary columns for reporting   label_binary(     labels = list(       GeoAreas_Locally = c("Serving locally", "Not serving locally"),       FinanceChng_Reserves = c("Drew on reserves", "Did not draw"),       PrgSrvc_Suspend = c("Paused services", "Did not pause")     ),     na_values = 97   ) %>%   label_binary(     labels = list(       ProgDem_Children = c("Serving children", "Not serving children"),       ProgDem_Elders = c("Serving seniors", "Not serving seniors")     )   ) %>%    # Step 9: Label Likert scales for reporting   label_likert(c("FinanceChng_Benefits", "FinanceChng_TotExp"),                labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase")) %>%   label_likert("FinanceChng_Salaries",                labels = c("Decrease in wages", "No change",                           "Increase in wages"))  glimpse(cleaned) #> Rows: 6 #> Columns: 32 #> $ GeoAreas_Local          <dbl> 1, 0, 0, NA, 0, 1 #> $ GeoAreas_MultipleLocal  <dbl> 0, 0, 1, NA, 0, 0 #> $ GeoAreas_RegionalWithin <dbl> 0, 0, 0, NA, 0, 0 #> $ GeoAreas_MultipleState  <dbl> 0, NA, 1, NA, 0, 0 #> $ GeoAreas_RegionalAcross <dbl> 0, NA, 0, NA, 0, 1 #> $ LLLost                  <dbl> 1, 0, 1, NA, 0, 1 #> $ LLDelay                 <dbl> 1, 1, 0, NA, 0, 1 #> $ LLStop                  <dbl> 0, 0, 0, NA, 0, 1 #> $ ProgDem_Children        <chr> "Not serving children", "Serving children", "S… #> $ ProgDem_Elders          <chr> "Serving seniors", "Not serving seniors", NA, … #> $ DonImportance           <dbl> 3, 1, NA, 2, NA, 4 #> $ PplSrv_NumWait          <dbl> 50, 0, 10, NA, 30, 0 #> $ PplSrv_NumWait_NA_X     <dbl> 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1 #> $ Staff_RegVlntr_2023     <dbl> 0, 5, NA, 10, 0, 3 #> $ Staff_RegVlntr_2024     <dbl> 2, NA, NA, 7, 0, 4 #> $ Staff_RegVlntr_NA       <dbl> 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0 #> $ FinanceChng_Benefits    <chr> "Decrease", "Decrease", "No change", "Increase… #> $ FinanceChng_Salaries    <chr> "Increase in wages", "Increase in wages", "No … #> $ FinanceChng_TotExp      <chr> "Decrease", "No change", "Unsure", "Increase",… #> $ FinanceChng_Reserves    <chr> "Drew on reserves", "Did not draw", "Did not d… #> $ PrgSrvc_Suspend         <chr> "Paused services", "Did not pause", "Did not p… #> $ Regulations             <dbl> 1, 0, 97, 1, 0, 1 #> $ Regulations_Federal     <dbl> 1, 0, 97, 0, 0, 1 #> $ Regulations_State       <dbl> 0, 0, 97, 1, 0, 1 #> $ HaveStaff               <dbl> 1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1 #> $ StaffVacancies          <dbl> 3, 1, NA, 0, NA, 1 #> $ BenefitsImpact          <dbl> 2, 3, NA, 4, NA, 3 #> $ GeoAreas_Locally        <chr> "Serving locally", "Not serving locally", "Ser… #> $ GeoAreas_Multistate     <int> 0, NA, 1, NA, 0, 1 #> $ LLDisruption_any        <int> 1, 1, 1, NA, 0, 1 #> $ LLDisruption_count      <int> 2, 1, 1, NA, 0, 3 #> $ LLDisruption_all        <int> 0, 0, 0, NA, 0, 1

title: Authors and Citation
text: Thiyaghessan Poongundranar. Author, maintainer. Urban Institute. Copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Poongundranar T (2026). ntistools: Tools Analyzing Nonprofit Trends Impacts Survey Data. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://urbaninstitute.github.io/ntistools/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ntistools: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data},   author = {Thiyaghessan Poongundranar},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://urbaninstitute.github.io/ntistools/}, }

what: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: ntistools provides reusable data-cleaning functions wrangling Nonprofit Trends Impacts Survey (NTIS) data. replaces repetitive dplyr::case_when() dplyr::across() patterns common NTIS analysis scripts purpose-built, pipe-friendly functions. Developed maintained Urban Institute.

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: Install development version GitHub: using remotes:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("UrbanInstitute/ntistools") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("UrbanInstitute/ntistools")

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: ntistools exports 10 functions organized four categories:

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: See vignette("--") side--side comparisons verbose case_when() code equivalent ntistools calls.
code: library(ntistools) library(dplyr)  cleaned <- raw_survey |>   recode_binary(c("ProgDem_Children", "ProgDem_Elders")) |>   recode_sentinel(c("DonImportance", "PrgSrvc_Suspend")) |>   impute_from_flag("PplSrv_NumWait") |>   combine_binary(GeoAreas_Locally,                  GeoAreas_Local, GeoAreas_MultipleLocal,                  GeoAreas_RegionalWithin) |>   collapse_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits") |>   label_binary(labels = list(     GeoAreas_Locally = c("Serving locally", "Not serving locally")   )) |>   label_likert("FinanceChng_Benefits",                labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase")) |>   propagate_parent(c("Regulations_Federal", "Regulations_State"),                    parent_var = "Regulations") |>   apply_filter(c("StaffVacancies", "BenefitsImpact"),                condition = HaveStaff == 1)

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: NTIS uses numeric sentinel values represent special responses: 98 = “Don’t know” 97 = “applicable” 0 = “None” (context-dependent) Many ntistools functions defaults reflect conventions (e.g., recode_sentinel() defaults replacing 98 NA, collapse_likert() treats 97 NA).

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: package maintained Urban Institute. find bug want suggest feature, please open issue.

title: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data
text: MIT

what: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
title: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
text: variable vars, values kept -condition TRUE set NA otherwise. useful filtering responses organizations answer certain questions.

title: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
code: apply_filter(data, vars, condition)

title: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names filter. condition unquoted expression evaluates logical vector. Rows TRUE keep values; rows get NA.

title: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
text: data filtered columns.

title: Conditionally set variables to NA when a condition is not met — apply_filter
code: d <- data.frame(keep = c(1, 0, 1), x = c(10, 20, 30)) apply_filter(d, "x", condition = keep == 1) #>   keep  x #> 1    1 10 #> 2    0 NA #> 3    1 30

title: Collapse Likert scales — collapse_likert
text: Recodes Likert-scale values using mapping vector. -th value mapping specifies original value become. Values na_values set NA.

title: Collapse Likert scales — collapse_likert
code: collapse_likert(data, vars, mapping = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 3L), na_values = 97)

title: Collapse Likert scales — collapse_likert
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names recode. mapping integer vector position gives new value original value . Defaults c(1, 1, 2, 3, 3) collapses 5-point Likert scale 3 categories. na_values Numeric vector values recode NA. Defaults 97.

title: Collapse Likert scales — collapse_likert
text: data recoded columns.

title: Collapse Likert scales — collapse_likert

what: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
title: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
text: Given several 0/1 columns, create new column 1 source column 1, NA source columns NA, 0 otherwise.

title: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
code: combine_binary(data, new_col, ..., strict_na = FALSE)

title: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
text: data data frame. new_col Unquoted name new column. ... Unquoted names binary (0/1) columns combine. strict_na Logical. TRUE, return NA source column NA (unless least one source 1). FALSE (default), return NA source columns NA.

title: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
text: data new column added.

title: Combine binary indicators with OR logic — combine_binary
code: d <- data.frame(a = c(0, 1, NA, 0), b = c(0, 0, NA, 1)) combine_binary(d, ab, a, b) #>    a  b ab #> 1  0  0  0 #> 2  1  0  1 #> 3 NA NA NA #> 4  0  1  1  # strict_na: NA poisons the result unless a 1 is present d2 <- data.frame(a = c(0, NA), b = c(NA, NA)) combine_binary(d2, ab, a, b, strict_na = TRUE) #>    a  b ab #> 1  0 NA NA #> 2 NA NA NA

what: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
title: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
text: Creates {prefix}_any (1 1), {prefix}_count (number 1s), {prefix}_all (1 1) set binary columns. three NA every source column NA.

title: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
code: count_binary(data, prefix, ...)

title: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
text: data data frame. prefix string used name output columns. ... Unquoted names binary (0/1) columns.

title: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
text: data three new columns added.

title: Count how many binary indicators are active — count_binary
code: d <- data.frame(x = c(1, 0, NA), y = c(1, 1, NA), z = c(0, 1, NA)) count_binary(d, "xyz", x, y, z) #>    x  y  z xyz_any xyz_count xyz_all #> 1  1  1  0       1         2       0 #> 2  0  1  1       1         2       0 #> 3 NA NA NA      NA        NA      NA

what: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
title: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
text: variable vars, looks corresponding flag column named {var}{flag_suffix}. original variable NA flag equals 1, value replaced impute_value (default 0).

title: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
code: impute_from_flag(   data,   vars,   flag_suffix = "_NA_X",   impute_value = 0,   flag_map = NULL )

title: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names impute. flag_suffix String appended variable name find flag column. Defaults "_NA_X". impute_value Value impute flag 1 variable NA. Defaults 0. flag_map Optional named character vector mapping variable names flag column names. provided, overrides paste0(var, flag_suffix) matched names. example, c("Staff_RegVlntr_2023" = "Staff_RegVlntr_NA").

title: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
text: data imputed values.

title: Replace NA with a value when a flag column indicates the skip is valid — impute_from_flag
code: d <- data.frame(x = c(1, NA, 3), x_NA_X = c(0, 1, 0)) impute_from_flag(d, "x") #>   x x_NA_X #> 1 1      0 #> 2 0      1 #> 3 3      0  # Using flag_map for non-standard flag column names d2 <- data.frame(val_2023 = c(NA, 5), val_flag = c(1, 0)) impute_from_flag(d2, "val_2023", flag_map = c(val_2023 = "val_flag")) #>   val_2023 val_flag #> 1        0        1 #> 2        5        0

what: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
title: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
text: Converts 0/1 columns character columns using named label pairs. Values true_values get first label, values false_values get second label, everything else becomes NA_character_.

title: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
code: label_binary(data, labels, true_values = 1, false_values = 0, na_values = NULL)

title: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
text: data data frame. labels named list element length-2 character vector c("true label", "false label"), keyed column name. true_values Numeric vector values map true (first) label. Defaults 1. false_values Numeric vector values map false (second) label. Defaults 0. na_values Optional numeric vector additional values map false label (e.g., 97 "applicable"). Defaults NULL.

title: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
text: data labeled columns.

title: Label binary columns with descriptive strings — label_binary
code: d <- data.frame(x = c(1, 0, NA, 97)) label_binary(d,   labels = list(x = c("Yes", "No")),   na_values = 97 ) #>      x #> 1  Yes #> 2   No #> 3 <NA> #> 4   No

what: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
title: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
text: Maps numeric Likert-scale values descriptive labels using mapping vector approach collapse_likert(), indexes labels produce character output. Values na_values mapped na_label instead NA.

title: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
code: label_likert(   data,   vars,   labels,   mapping = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 3L),   na_values = 97,   na_label = "Unsure" )

title: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names label. labels character vector labels, one per collapsed category. Length must equal max(mapping). example, c("Decrease", "change", "Increase"). mapping integer vector position gives collapsed category original value . Defaults c(1L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 3L). na_values Numeric vector values map na_label. Defaults 97. na_label Character string used values na_values. Defaults "Unsure".

title: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
text: data labeled columns.

title: Label Likert-scale columns with descriptive strings — label_likert
code: d <- data.frame(q1 = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 97)) label_likert(d, "q1",   labels = c("Decrease", "No change", "Increase") ) #>          q1 #> 1  Decrease #> 2  Decrease #> 3 No change #> 4  Increase #> 5  Increase #> 6    Unsure

what: ntistools: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data — ntistools-package
title: ntistools: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data — ntistools-package
text: Reusable functions wrangling, analyzing, visualizing Nonprofit Trends Impacts Survey (NTIS) data. package internal use contain raw survey data PII.

title: ntistools: Tools for Analyzing Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey Data — ntistools-package
text: Maintainer: Thiyaghessan Poongundranar tpoongundranar@urban.org contributors: Urban Institute [copyright holder]

what: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
title: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
text: parent variable one specified values (e.g., 0 97), set value child columns. useful parent question gates set follow-questions.

title: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
code: propagate_parent(data, vars, parent_var, values = c(0, 97))

title: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
text: data data frame. vars character vector child column names. parent_var string giving name parent column. values Numeric vector parent values propagate children. Defaults c(0, 97).

title: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
text: data child columns updated.

title: Push parent variable's special values to child columns — propagate_parent
code: d <- data.frame(parent = c(0, 1, 97, 1), child1 = c(NA, 1, NA, 0)) propagate_parent(d, "child1", parent_var = "parent", values = c(0, 97)) #>   parent child1 #> 1      0      0 #> 2      1      1 #> 3     97     97 #> 4      1      0

title: Collapse categories into 0/1 — recode_binary
text: Recodes set columns values ones become 1, values zeros become 0, values na_values become NA.

title: Collapse categories into 0/1 — recode_binary
code: recode_binary(data, vars, ones = c(1, 2), zeros = 0, na_values = 98)

title: Collapse categories into 0/1 — recode_binary
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names recode. ones Numeric vector values recode 1. Defaults c(1, 2). zeros Numeric vector values recode 0. Defaults 0. na_values Numeric vector values recode NA. Defaults 98.

title: Collapse categories into 0/1 — recode_binary
text: data recoded columns.

title: Collapse categories into 0/1 — recode_binary
code: d <- data.frame(x = c(0, 1, 2, 98)) recode_binary(d, "x") #>    x #> 1  0 #> 2  1 #> 3  1 #> 4 NA

what: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
title: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
text: Replaces specified sentinel values (e.g., 98 "know") NA.

title: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
code: recode_sentinel(data, vars, values = 98)

title: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names. values Numeric vector sentinel values replace NA. Defaults 98.

title: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
text: data sentinel values replaced NA.

title: Replace sentinel values with NA — recode_sentinel
code: d <- data.frame(x = c(1, 98, 3), y = c(98, 2, 98)) recode_sentinel(d, c("x", "y"), values = 98) #>    x  y #> 1  1 NA #> 2 NA  2 #> 3  3 NA
